title: advance_diversifier_to rolls back an empty SQLite transaction on no-op path
description: db.rs advance_diversifier_to: when idx <= current, the function returns Ok(()) without calling tx.commit(). SQLite rolls back the empty transaction on drop. This is functionally correct but wastes a transaction begin + rollback on the fast path. In send_payment this can be called even when actual_u128 == change_di_start (no gap to close). The guard 'if actual_u128 > change_di_start' in payment.rs prevents the call most of the time, but alloc_fresh_address always calls advance_diversifier_to even on the no-gap path and that function does the transaction unconditionally. Low impact — SQLite WAL transactions are cheap — but worth noting.
